rationale: Watchlist is saturated with AI mega-caps (AAPL/MSFT/GOOGL/NVDA) plus broad-market SPY, with zero exposure to the profile's explicit next waves (robotics, rockets/spacecraft, nuclear). ARKQ (ARK Autonomous Technology & Robotics ETF) is the most direct, liquid way to advance the 'invest early in next waves while cheap' rule. Holdings span industrial robotics, autonomous vehicles, 3D printing, and space/aerospace names — covering two of the profile's next-wave buckets in one ticker. ADV well above the $5M liquidity floor.

SPY's largest weights at this date are AAPL, MSFT, AMZN, FB, GOOGL, NVDA — the watchlist already holds the top mega-caps directly, so SPY is the most redundant slot. Removing it frees a slot for thematic next-wave exposure that the rest of the watchlist cannot provide. The optimizer can still allocate AAPL/MSFT/GOOGL/NVDA directly.

Make room for ARKX while staying at cap=5. ARKQ's robotics/autonomy exposure overlaps heavily with the four AI mega-caps already held (NVDA/MSFT/GOOGL/AAPL all sit inside the autonomy supply chain), so removing it is the lowest-conviction trim. The freed slot funds a missing wave (rockets/spacecraft) rather than doubling-up on AI-adjacent autonomy.

Pure-play uranium/nuclear-fuel ETF (Global X, US-listed, liquid). The thesis names nuclear (SMRs + AI data-center PPAs) as a target next wave and the watchlist currently has zero nuclear exposure. Uranium just printed its strongest rally in years on tight supply (Kazatomprom holding 2022-2023 production flat, Cameco cutting McArthur River guidance) plus Sprott's new physical trust absorbing spot supply — a structural-deficit setup, not a meme spike. URA captures the miners and converters that benefit before any SMR developer is investable in public markets.

Pure-play small-launch and space-systems company; began trading on Nasdaq via Vector SPAC merger on Aug 25, 2021 (pre-as-of-date, eligible). The profile calls out rockets as a next wave and the only current rocket exposure is ARKX, whose largest holdings are GPS/construction-machinery names (Trimble, Komatsu) rather than launch providers. RKLB is the cleanest investable proxy for the launch-cadence thesis in late 2021 — Electron is the second-most-frequently-launched US rocket and Neutron has just been announced.

Robotics & AI ETF (Global X, US-listed, liquid). Profile lists robotics as a next wave and the watchlist has no robotics-specific exposure. BOTZ's largest names beyond NVDA are industrial-automation pure-plays (Keyence, Fanuc, Mitsubishi Electric, ABB) that capture the falling-cost-of-industrial-robots trend at a portfolio level — cleaner than picking one Japanese ADR. NVDA overlap is acceptable: BOTZ adds the industrial-automation tail behind the AI-compute leader I already hold.

Trimming one AI mega-cap to fund a nuclear slot. With cap=5 and four AI names (AAPL/MSFT/GOOGL/NVDA) crowding the book, GOOGL is the least levered to the specific wave-thesis catalysts (cloud-AI infra, AI compute, on-device AI); MSFT (Azure + early OpenAI partnership) and NVDA (training silicon) carry the AI thesis more directly. Consistent with the profile's 'ride AI wave, trim before crest' guidance — not exiting AI, just concentrating it.

Trimming a second AI mega-cap to fund the robotics slot. AAPL is consumer-electronics-heavy and the least exposed to data-center AI compute, cloud AI services, and industrial automation. Keeping MSFT (cloud AI, productivity AI) and NVDA (the picks-and-shovels AI compute leader) preserves the AI-wave conviction while opening room for the rockets/robotics/nuclear early bets the profile explicitly calls for.

Profile names quantum as a target next wave and the watchlist has zero quantum exposure. IonQ became the first publicly traded, pure-play quantum computing company on Oct 1, 2021 (pre-as-of-date, eligible) via the dMY Technology Group III SPAC merger, raising $636M to fund commercialization of trapped-ion quantum systems. Three months of trading history make it US-listed and liquid. No competing pure-play exists in Q4 2021 (Rigetti/IBM-quantum/Honeywell-Quantinuum either pre-merger or buried inside conglomerates), and QTUM is a thematic ETF that dilutes pure-play exposure. This is the cleanest investable proxy for the quantum thesis the profile explicitly calls out.

Replaced by IONQ to open a quantum slot at the cap=5 ceiling. BOTZ is the lowest-conviction holding for two reasons: (1) the robotics wave is already partially captured by NVDA (which sits in BOTZ's top holdings and supplies the GPUs/Jetson modules underlying most modern autonomy stacks), so BOTZ overlap with NVDA is real; (2) BOTZ's industrial-automation tail (Keyence, Fanuc, Mitsubishi Electric) is mostly Japanese ADRs trading on a slower, deeper-cycle dynamic than the AI/quantum/space/nuclear catalysts the profile is built around. Trading a partially-redundant diversified ETF for the first liquid pure-play in a profile-named next wave is the higher-leverage move. Net effect: watchlist still has four next-wave bets (RKLB rockets, URA nuclear, IONQ quantum, plus NVDA covering robotics-via-AI-compute) and two AI anchors (MSFT, NVDA).

The profile names 'geopolitical realignment driving energy prices' as an active non-tech wave to watch; Russia's Feb 24 invasion of Ukraine is the in-window trigger that turned this from a watch into a position, and the watchlist currently has zero direct fossil-energy exposure (URA captures only the uranium-via-Rosatom-sanctions leg). XLE is the cleanest, most liquid US-listed proxy: the Energy Select Sector SPDR ETF (launched 1998, ~$1B+ ADV) is market-cap-weighted into XOM and CVX (>40% combined), so one slot at cap=5 buys the integrated-oil-major reflex to $100+ Brent and the Biden Mar 8 executive order banning Russian oil/gas/coal imports. Not in profile exclusions (only solar/wind are excluded).

At cap=5 the geopolitical-energy slot can only be opened by removing one current name, and MSFT is the lowest-conviction holding relative to its thesis. The profile says ride AI but trim before crest, and at cap=5 the watchlist does not need two mega-cap AI anchors — NVDA is the more concentrated AI-compute pure-play (every hyperscaler's training cluster is its silicon), while MSFT's Q1-2022 narrative is dominated by the Jan 18 announcement of a $68.7B all-cash deal to acquire Activision Blizzard, a gaming-consolidation story carrying material FTC/CMA regulatory risk and a multi-year close window — not an AI catalyst. Keeping NVDA preserves the AI anchor; rotating MSFT into XLE adds the live geopolitical-energy wave the profile names verbatim. URA, RKLB, IONQ stay — each is the sole watchlist representative of a profile-named next wave (nuclear, rockets, quantum) with a fresh in-window catalyst (uranium Mar 10 spike to $60+/lb on Rosatom sanction fears; Neutron Wallops/MARS site announcement Feb 28; IONQ Forte ytterbium-system roadmap).

IONQ is the lowest-conviction holding in the 90d lookback. Quantum remains the most distant of the named tech waves with no commercial-revenue catalyst in Q3 2022; IONQ posted a Q2 2022 (Aug 15) report with revenue still in the single-digit millions and a widening net loss. With the broader risk-off environment (Fed hiked 75bp on 2022-09-21, third consecutive 75bp move) hammering long-duration unprofitable tech the hardest, IONQ is the holding most exposed to multiple compression and the one I'd cycle out of to make room for a dated-catalyst non-tech name. Quantum thesis is paused, not abandoned, and IONQ can be re-added later when a commercial milestone lands.

Opens an explicit robotics-wave slot named in the profile thesis; BOTZ provides diversified exposure to industrial robotics, automation, and AI-adjacent names that complement single-stock NVDA without doubling the AI bucket.

Energy bucket is overweighted (XLE + LNG + URA = 3 of 5 slots); XLE overlaps LNG inside the geopolitical-energy thesis while crude has pulled back sharply (Brent fell ~$16 to ~$73/b between mid-April and early May 2023), eroding the broad-oil tailwind. LNG remains the cleaner Russia-Ukraine LNG-export bet, so dropping XLE frees a slot for the uncovered robotics wave without abandoning the geopolitical-energy thesis.

Pure-play quantum-computing exposure at a commercial inflection: IonQ disclosed it had reached its three-year $100M cumulative-bookings target ahead of plan, the only watchlist gap against the profile's named next-waves (rockets covered by RKLB, nuclear by URA, robotics by BOTZ, AI by NVDA).

At a 5-name watchlist cap, a broad robotics ETF is the weakest slot to hold: the robotics wave is still slow-buildup, BOTZ's top holdings overlap NVDA exposure already in the list, and freeing the slot lets a pure-play next-wave name (quantum) enter without violating the cap.

Robotics is named in the profile as a next wave and Q1-2024 has the first commercial-grade catalysts: Figure AI's $675M raise from MSFT/NVDA/Bezos/OpenAI (Feb 29) and NVIDIA GTC's Project GR00T/Isaac humanoid platform (Mar 18) put the buildup phase on the clock.

Quantum stays in the profile but at cap=5 it must yield to a more commercially mature wave; IONQ remains pre-revenue with no near-term inflection, while robotics has named hyperscaler money flowing this quarter.

Fills the long-open quantum gap with a concrete commercial milestone: IonQ announced a $1.075B definitive agreement to acquire Oxford Ionics on June 9, 2025, consolidating trapped-ion fidelity records and accelerating its hardware roadmap, while Q1 2025 revenue had already beat guidance.

At cap=5, BOTZ is the weakest holding to free a slot: it is a broad robotics/AI ETF with material NVDA overlap (already expressed directly via NVDA), and no concrete Q2 2025 robotics catalyst surfaced inside the lookback to justify keeping it over a pure-play quantum entry that the profile explicitly flags as a named next wave.
